ingredients.name: Chuou Ichiba dori
ingredients.address: Chuou Ichiba dori, Chuo City, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Tsukiji-area market street appears in episode 5 as a backdrop during the Armed Detective Agency's movements through Tokyo, establishing the grittier urban contrast to Yokohama. The street's bustling atmosphere grounds the series' blend of literary fiction and noir action.

ingredients.name: Yamashita Park
ingredients.address: 279 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yamashita Park is one of the most iconic pilgrimage spots for BSD fans, appearing in episode 5 during the Guild War arc as Atsushi and his allies navigate Yokohama's waterfront under threat from Fitzgerald's forces. The seaside promenade's panoramic view of the harbor mirrors the series' sweeping stakes as the city itself becomes a battleground.

ingredients.name: Starbucks Coffee - Yokohama Motomachi
ingredients.localName: スターバックスコーヒー 横浜元町店
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
ingredients.address: ラヴィ元町 1F, 1 Chome-13-5 Motomachi, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0861, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Motomachi branch appears in episode 13 during the tense final stretch of the Guild arc, with the Motomachi shopping street serving as a recognizable civilian setting that highlights the human cost of the supernatural conflict engulfing Yokohama. Fans spot the distinctive storefront lining Motomachi's European-styled avenue.

ingredients.name: Canton Hanten
ingredients.address: Canton Hanten, 144 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Cantonese restaurant in Yokohama's Chinatown district appears in the opening sequence of episode 5, establishing the Guild arc's Yokohama Chinatown backdrop where characters move through crowded streets. Its location near Yamashita Park places it at the heart of the show's pilgrimage cluster.

ingredients.name: No.1 Dock Top Gate
ingredients.localName: No.1 Dock トップゲート(扉船)
ingredients.entityContext: This industrial dock gate near Yokohama's port appears in the opening credits of the Guild arc episodes, its massive iron structure evoking the harbor-city atmosphere that defines the Port Mafia's domain. The gate's imposing silhouette is a recurring visual shorthand for Yokohama's underworld geography.

ingredients.name: Port No. 3 Bridge
ingredients.localName: 港三号橋梁 (旧大岡川橋梁)
ingredients.entityContext: The old Oka River railroad bridge appears in the ending credits of episode 5, its weathered iron framework against the Yokohama waterfront providing one of the most atmospheric pilgrimage shots in the series. Fans recreate this exact angle where the rusted trestle frames the harbor.

ingredients.name: Osanbashi Wharf
ingredients.address: Osanbashi Pier, 大さん橋ふ頭ビル前桟橋 1 Chome-１-4 Kaigandori, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yokohama's iconic cruise terminal appears in the episode 5 ending, its sweeping wooden deck and harbor views serving as a dramatic backdrop during the Guild War arc. The wharf's distinctive wave-shaped roof and open water vista make it one of the most recognizable BSD location shots in Yokohama.

ingredients.name: Bar Lupin
ingredients.address: Bar Lupin, 塚本不動産ビル 地下 5 Chome-5-11 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0061, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Bar Lupin in Ginza is the storied real-world haunt of author Osamu Dazai, and the anime honors this literary connection in episode 1 by using it as the setting where Dazai Osamu (named after the author) drinks and plots, tying the character's melancholic persona directly to his real-life namesake's favorite bar.

ingredients.name: Yokohama Museum of Art
ingredients.address: Yokohama Museum of Art, 横浜美術館 内 3 Chome-4-1 Minatomirai, Nishi Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 220-0012, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Minatomirai museum appears in episode 3 as a recognizable landmark during the Guild arc, with its grand neoclassical facade visible as characters move through the Minato Mirai district. The museum's landmark status in Yokohama makes it a natural anchor for the series' lovingly rendered cityscape.

ingredients.name: Grand Mall Park
ingredients.address: Grand Mall Park, 3 Chome-5 Minatomirai, Nishi Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 220-0012, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Grand Mall Park adjacent to the Yokohama Museum of Art appears in episode 3, its long tree-lined promenade providing a serene contrast to the supernatural chaos of the Guild arc. The park's fountain plaza is a documented pilgrimage location that fans photograph alongside the museum backdrop.

ingredients.name: Kanagawa Prefectural Shonan Coast Park
ingredients.address: Kanagawa Prefectural Shonan Coast Park, 1 Chome-17-3 Kugenumakaigan, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0037, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shonan coastline park in Fujisawa appears in episode 3, its sandy beach and Pacific Ocean horizon used to depict a quieter moment for Atsushi as the weight of the Guild War begins to press on the Agency. The wide coastal view evokes the vulnerability of Yokohama's civilians caught between supernatural factions.

ingredients.name: Kugenuma Bridge
ingredients.address: Kugenuma Bridge, マイキャッスル鵠沼海岸 2 Chome-17-16 Kugenumakaigan, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0037, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kugenuma Bridge over the Hikiji River in Fujisawa appears in episode 4 during an interlude scene, its modest concrete span and surrounding coastal neighborhood grounding the series in recognizable suburban Kanagawa. Pilgrims visit to photograph the bridge angle that matches the anime's framing.

ingredients.name: Yokohama District Court
ingredients.address: Yokohama District Court, ９ Nihonodori, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-8502, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Yokohama District Court on Nihonodori appears in episode 5 during the Guild arc's escalating conflict, its imposing government building facade lending authority to scenes where legal and criminal worlds collide. The court's proximity to the waterfront places it squarely in the show's Kannai district pilgrimage route.

ingredients.name: MARK IS Minatomirai
ingredients.localName: MARK IS みなとみらい
ingredients.address: MARK IS Minatomirai, 3 Chome-5-1 Minatomirai, Nishi Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 220-0012, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The large shopping complex in Minato Mirai appears in episode 6, its distinctive cylindrical towers visible as a Yokohama skyline marker during Guild arc action sequences. The complex anchors the Minato Mirai pilgrimage cluster alongside the nearby Yokohama Museum of Art and Grand Mall Park.

ingredients.name: Shinko Bridge
ingredients.address: Shinkyō Bridge, Kamihatsuishimachi, Nikko, Tochigi 321-1401, Japan

ingredients.name: Yamashita Rinko Line Promenade
ingredients.localName: 山下臨港線プロムナード
ingredients.address: Yamashita Rinko Line Promenade, 1 Chome Kaigandori, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This elevated promenade built on the old Yamashita Rinko railroad line appears in episode 8, its industrial catwalks overlooking the Yokohama harbor providing a dramatic vantage point during the Guild War's most intense clashes. The promenade's unique position between the city and the sea makes it one of the most photographed BSD locations in Yokohama.

ingredients.name: Times station Yokohama Yamashita-cho
ingredients.localName: タイムズステーション横浜山下町
ingredients.address: Times station Yokohama Yamashita-cho, 28 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The parking facility in Yamashita-cho appears in episode 8 as a background location in the Chinatown district during the Guild arc's Yokohama Chinatown sequences. Its street-level signage matches pilgrimage comparison shots circulated in Japanese fan communities.

ingredients.name: Beijing Fandian
ingredients.localName: 横浜中華街 北京飯店
ingredients.entityContext: This Yokohama Chinatown restaurant appears in episode 8 during the Guild War arc's Chinatown sequences, with the restaurant's neon-lit exterior serving as part of the dense, atmospheric street scenery the series uses to convey the Port Mafia's territorial hold over the district.

ingredients.name: Lawson Yamashitacho Honmachi Dori store
ingredients.localName: ローソン 横浜不老町一丁目店
ingredients.address: LAWSON Yokohama Furocho 1 Chome Store, 1 Chome-1-5 Furocho, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The convenience store near Yamashita-cho appears in episode 8 as a background location during street-level scenes in the Yokohama Chinatown area, its familiar blue signage providing an everyday civilian anchor amid the supernatural conflict. BSD's careful use of real Yokohama storefronts is a hallmark of the series' location work.

ingredients.name: America Yama Park
ingredients.address: America Yama Park, 97-1 Yamatecho, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0862, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: America Yama Park atop the hill above Motomachi appears in episode 9, its garden terrace and view over the rooftops of Naka Ward serving as a quiet meeting point in the Guild arc's later episodes. The park's connection to the former American consul site adds a layer of historical atmosphere fitting the series' Meiji-era literary aesthetics.

ingredients.name: Windmills in the French Mountains
ingredients.entityContext: The decorative windmills in France-yama (French Hill) park in Yamate appear in episode 9, this romantic hilltop garden used for a character scene amid the escalating Guild arc. The windmills are a beloved Yokohama landmark and a documented BSD pilgrimage stop that fans photograph to match the anime's composition.

ingredients.name: Statue of the Girl with Red Shoes
ingredients.localName: 赤い靴はいていた女の子像
ingredients.address: Statue of the Girl with Red Shoes, Yamashitachō, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The bronze statue of the girl in red shoes near Yamashita Park appears in episode 12, a quiet and poignant landmark used during the emotional climax of the Guild arc as Atsushi reflects on the cost of the battle for Yokohama. The statue, based on a famous Japanese children's poem about a girl taken abroad, resonates with BSD's themes of lost innocence and longing.

ingredients.name: Daily Yamazaki Motomachi Chukagai Station
ingredients.localName: ニューヤマザキデイリーストア 元町中華街駅店
ingredients.address: Daily Yamazaki, 1 Chome-１１ Motomachi, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0861, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The convenience store at Motomachi-Chukagai Station appears in episode 13 during the final Guild arc scenes, part of the everyday urban texture BSD uses to ground its supernatural drama in recognizable Yokohama street life. Fans photograph the storefront to compare with the anime's background art.

ingredients.name: Motomachi-Chukagai Station
ingredients.localName: 元町・中華街 (山下公園)駅
ingredients.address: Motomachi-Chukagai Station, Yamashitachō, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Motomachi-Chukagai Station on the Minatomirai Line appears in episode 13 as characters move through the Motomachi area during the Guild arc's conclusion, its distinctive underground platform and street-level exits recognizable to anyone who has made the BSD pilgrimage to this corner of Yokohama. The station serves as the transit hub for the show's densest cluster of pilgrimage locations.

ingredients.name: Aipurimo Yokohamamotomachiten
ingredients.localName: アイプリモ 横浜元町店
ingredients.address: Aipurimo Yokohamamotomachiten, 1 Chome-１１-5 Motomachi, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0861, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The jewelry store on Motomachi's main shopping street appears in episode 13 as part of the Motomachi streetscape during the Guild arc finale, its upscale storefront contributing to the European-styled Motomachi atmosphere the anime captures in its background art. BSD fans visit Motomachi to walk the same shopping street depicted in this episode.
